quiz.title: Unit 8 Quiz 3, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Mass from Molarity and Millimole Calculations
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Calculating Mass of Solute from Molarity and Volume, Millimoles, Conversions between Moles and Millimoles

questions.question:
The mass of NaOH present in 200 cm³ of a 0.5 M solution is:
(Molar mass of NaOH = 40 g/mol)
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Convert volume to litres

200 cm³ = 0.200 L

Step 2: Calculate moles

Moles = M × volume in L = 0.5 × 0.200 = 0.1 mol

Step 3: Calculate mass

Mass = moles × molar mass = 0.1 × 40 = 4 g

questions.question:
The mass of NaCl present in 250 cm³ of a 1 M solution is:
(Molar mass of NaCl = 58.5 g/mol)
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Convert volume to litres

250 cm³ = 0.250 L

Step 2: Calculate moles

Moles = 1 × 0.250 = 0.25 mol

Step 3: Calculate mass

Mass = 0.25 × 58.5 = 14.625 ≈ 14.6 g

questions.question:
The mass of KNO₃ present in 500 cm³ of a 0.2 M solution is:
(Molar mass = 101 g/mol)
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Convert volume to litres

500 cm³ = 0.500 L

Step 2: Calculate moles

Moles = 0.2 × 0.500 = 0.1 mol

Step 3: Calculate mass

Mass = 0.1 × 101 = 10.1 g

questions.question:
The mass of H₂SO₄ present in 100 cm³ of a 1 M solution is:
(Molar mass = 98 g/mol)
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Convert volume to litres

100 cm³ = 0.100 L

Step 2: Calculate moles

Moles = 1 × 0.100 = 0.1 mol

Step 3: Calculate mass

Mass = 0.1 × 98 = 9.8 g

questions.question:
The mass of CaCl₂ present in 200 cm³ of a 0.25 M solution is:
(Molar mass = 111 g/mol)
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Convert volume to litres

200 cm³ = 0.200 L

Step 2: Calculate moles

Moles = 0.25 × 0.200 = 0.05 mol

Step 3: Calculate mass

Mass = 0.05 × 111 = 5.55 g

questions.question:
The mass of Na₂CO₃ present in 500 cm³ of a 0.1 M solution is:
(Molar mass = 106 g/mol)
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Convert volume to litres

500 cm³ = 0.500 L

Step 2: Calculate moles

Moles = 0.1 × 0.500 = 0.05 mol

Step 3: Calculate mass

Mass = 0.05 × 106 = 5.3 g

questions.question:
The mass of HCl present in 250 cm³ of a 2 M solution is:
(Molar mass = 36.5 g/mol)
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Convert volume to litres

250 cm³ = 0.250 L

Step 2: Calculate moles

Moles = 2 × 0.250 = 0.5 mol

Step 3: Calculate mass

Mass = 0.5 × 36.5 = 18.25 g

questions.question:
The mass of glucose present in 100 cm³ of a 0.5 M solution is:
(Molar mass = 180 g/mol)
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Convert volume to litres

100 cm³ = 0.100 L

Step 2: Calculate moles

Moles = 0.5 × 0.100 = 0.05 mol

Step 3: Calculate mass

Mass = 0.05 × 180 = 9 g

questions.question:
The mass of KOH present in 400 cm³ of a 0.5 M solution is:
(Molar mass = 56 g/mol)
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Convert volume to litres

400 cm³ = 0.400 L

Step 2: Calculate moles

Moles = 0.5 × 0.400 = 0.2 mol

Step 3: Calculate mass

Mass = 0.2 × 56 = 11.2 g

questions.question:
The mass of NH₄Cl present in 200 cm³ of a 1.5 M solution is:
(Molar mass = 53.5 g/mol)
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Convert volume to litres

200 cm³ = 0.200 L

Step 2: Calculate moles

Moles = 1.5 × 0.200 = 0.3 mol

Step 3: Calculate mass

Mass = 0.3 × 53.5 = 16.05 g

questions.question: The number of millimoles present in 200 cm³ of a 2.0 M solution is:
questions.explanation:
Method 1: Calculate moles first

Volume in L = 0.200 L
Moles = 2.0 × 0.200 = 0.4 mol
Millimoles = 0.4 × 1000 = 400 mmol

Method 2: Direct formula

Millimoles = M × volume in mL = 2.0 × 200 = 400 mmol

questions.question: The number of millimoles present in 500 cm³ of a 0.5 M solution is:
questions.explanation:
Direct formula:

Millimoles = M × volume in mL = 0.5 × 500 = 250 mmol

questions.question: The number of millimoles present in 100 cm³ of a 1 M solution is:
questions.explanation:
Direct formula:

Millimoles = M × volume in mL = 1 × 100 = 100 mmol

questions.question: The number of millimoles present in 250 cm³ of a 0.4 M solution is:
questions.explanation:
Direct formula:

Millimoles = M × volume in mL = 0.4 × 250 = 100 mmol

questions.question: The number of millimoles present in 50 cm³ of a 2 M solution is:
questions.explanation:
Direct formula:

Millimoles = M × volume in mL = 2 × 50 = 100 mmol

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered mass and millimole calculations! 🧪⚖️
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the relationship between moles, mass, and molar mass.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on converting between cm³ and L correctly.
scoreMessages.0: Please review the formula: moles = M × volume (L), then mass = moles × molar mass.
